steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle A
steps.content: Angle A is at the bottom left corner. The side adjacent to angle A is $AC = 41$. The hypotenuse (opposite the right angle at C) is $AB = 42$.

steps.title: Calculate cos A
steps.content: Substitute the adjacent side and hypotenuse: $$\cos A = \frac{41}{42}$$ B is correct.
